resources.string: This application was developed by ELATEC, A Brand of Allegion (Jason Ouellette), with original contributions from Johnson Controls International (Thank you to Luke Ruter!). It demonstrates PKOC, Aliro, and LEAF Verified credential protocols for physical access control over NFC and BLE.

PKOC (Public Key Open Credential) is a free, open specification developed by the Physical Security Interoperability Alliance (PSIA). It enables secure, interoperable access credentials using asymmetric public key cryptography — with no fees, royalties, or membership requirements. The private key never leaves the device; the public key serves as the credential.

Aliro is an open access credential specification developed by the Connectivity Standards Alliance (CSA) — the same organization behind Matter and Zigbee. Aliro defines a standardized, cryptographically secure protocol for NFC and BLE-based physical access control, enabling interoperability across devices, readers, and ecosystems. This application implements Aliro 1.0 for both NFC and BLE (L2CAP CoC) transports.
ELATEC is a Brand of Allegion — a global pioneer in security products and solutions for homes and businesses.

LEAF Verified is a finished physical access credential built on NXP MIFARE DUOX silicon (Common Criteria EAL 6+). It uses ECC P-256 cryptography and X.509 PKI certificates over NFC (ISO 14443 Type A, ISO 7816-4 APDU). The 12-digit Open ID is a globally unique identifier provisioned at wafer fabrication and cryptographically bound to the credential\u2019s certificate. Authentication follows Path 1 (Open Application): the reader verifies the credential\u2019s certificate against the LEAF Root CA, issues a 32-byte challenge, and the credential signs it with its hardware-locked private key. The verified Open ID is output as a 40-bit Wiegand frame (38 data + 2 parity bits) for compatibility with legacy access control panels.
version: BLE-4.0, NFC-1.63 · Aliro 1.0 · LEAF Verified 1.0
This image is a closed door.
Please present device to reader to send credential
NFC is disabled. Please enable it if you intend to use NFC to transmit credentials.
Enter Site Public Key In Uncompressed Format
Place a barcode inside the viewfinder rectangle to scan it.
Enter the verification code sent to your email
Please enter a valid email address
Invalid verification code. Please try again.
Verification code expired. Please request a new one.
%s needs your permission to use smartphone access
Select the email address the organisation knows you by
Failed to load credentials. Please try again.
%s needs your permission to use smartphone access
To use Sentry Interactive Mobile Access at buildings managed by %s you need to agree to sharing the following data.
Consenting doesn\'t automatically mean you\'ll have access

No organisation in context. Get an invite code to start with Sentry Interactive
